"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command '$src = Get-ChildItem -Path (Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE '"'.codex\\generated_images') -Recurse -Filter 'ig_*.png' | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
if (-not "'$src) { throw '"'No generated image found under .codex\\generated_images' }
"'$dest = Join-Path $env:OD_PROJECT_DIR '"'expandly-poster-faq-logo-corners.png'
Copy-Item -LiteralPath "'$src.FullName -Destination $dest -Force
$item = Get-Item -LiteralPath $dest
[pscustomobject]@{ source=$src.FullName; dest=$item.FullName; size=$item.Length; exists=$true } | ConvertTo-Json -Compress'

content:
I’ll use the attached reference as the logo-corner target and revise the latest blue/orange FAQ poster so the corner mark matches that stronger Expandly bracket treatment.
The reference shows the corner treatment clearly: blue L-brackets on the left, orange L-brackets on the right, with the small blue/orange Expandly bracket mark next to the wordmark. I’ll keep that exact logo logic and avoid generic decorative corners.
This pass matches the requested corner language: four outer L-brackets in blue/orange plus the inner Expandly bracket mark near the wordmark. I’m copying the generated PNG into the project with a new versioned filename now.The image is now verified in the project folder as `expandly-poster-faq-logo-corners.png`. I’m updating the critique note to point at this version and record the logo-corner correction.Done. I created the revised poster with the Expandly-style corner logo treatment from your reference:

`expandly-poster-faq-logo-corners.png`

It uses blue L-corners on the left, orange L-corners on the right, and keeps the small blue/orange Expandly bracket mark near the wordmark. I also updated `critique.json` to point to this version.
